1	"""Decision frames — orthogonal lenses on a candidate trade.
2	
3	Each frame is a small, pure function over :class:`FrameContext` that
4	returns a :class:`FrameResult`. A frame describes:
5	
6	- the *name* of the lens it applies (e.g. "incentive_alignment");
7	- the *inputs* it consumes (which metrics, which context fields);
8	- the *assumptions* the lens rests on (and whether they look stable
9	  in this context);
10	- a verdict (:class:`FrameVerdict`) — the lens's standalone vote;
11	- the *failure modes* the frame is on the lookout for (purely
12	  descriptive — used in the trace).
13	
14	The frame layer deliberately does not synthesize. It only emits
15	results. :func:`noosphere.decisions.synthesis.synthesize` is the only
16	place where multiple frames are combined into a single action — and
17	that combiner is itself a deterministic rule, not a model.
18	
19	Why split this up at all? The architecture contract in
20	``docs/architecture/Algorithmized_Decision_Making.md`` (§1.2 and §2.5)
21	wants the trace to be *the* artifact: prose flows from the trace, not
22	the other way around. Multiple inspectable frames make the trace
23	genuinely inspectable — a reader can ask "which lenses agreed?" and
24	"which one blocked?" without reverse-engineering one collapsed score.
25	"""
26	
27	from __future__ import annotations
28	
29	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
30	from enum import Enum
31	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping, Sequence
32	
33	
34	FRAMES_VERSION = "decision_frames@v1"
35	
36	
37	class FrameVerdict(str, Enum):
38	    """A single frame's vote on the candidate trade.
39	
40	    Ordering of severity (low → high) when synthesis breaks ties:
41	
42	    - ``SUPPORT``  — frame is comfortable with the trade as proposed.
43	    - ``WATCH``    — frame sees enough signal to surface, not stake.
44	    - ``ABSTAIN``  — frame's assumptions don't hold in this context.
45	    - ``REDUCE``   — frame is calling for less exposure (existing position).
46	    - ``EXIT``     — frame is calling for full closure (existing position).
47	    - ``HEDGE``    — frame is calling for offsetting exposure.
48	    - ``HARD_STOP``— frame considers the trade categorically forbidden
49	                     here; ``HARD_STOP`` from any frame forces
50	                     ``ABSTAIN`` at synthesis regardless of agreement
51	                     elsewhere.
52	    """
53	
54	    SUPPORT = "SUPPORT"
55	    WATCH = "WATCH"
56	    ABSTAIN = "ABSTAIN"
57	    REDUCE = "REDUCE"
58	    EXIT = "EXIT"
59	    HEDGE = "HEDGE"
60	    HARD_STOP = "HARD_STOP"
61	
62	
63	_VERDICT_RANK = {
64	    FrameVerdict.SUPPORT: 0,
65	    FrameVerdict.WATCH: 1,
66	    FrameVerdict.ABSTAIN: 2,
67	    FrameVerdict.REDUCE: 3,
68	    FrameVerdict.EXIT: 4,
69	    FrameVerdict.HEDGE: 5,
70	    FrameVerdict.HARD_STOP: 6,
71	}
72	
73	
74	def verdict_severity(verdict: FrameVerdict) -> int:
75	    return _VERDICT_RANK[verdict]
76	
77	
78	# Thresholds the frames consult. Held here (not env-driven) so the
79	# decision trace is reproducible from its inputs alone.
80	CONTRADICTION_HARD_STOP = 0.5
81	CONTRADICTION_WATCH = 0.25
82	REFLEXIVITY_DECAY_WATCH = 0.5
83	REFLEXIVITY_EDGE_DOWNGRADE = 0.15
84	OPTION_VALUE_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR = 0.45
85	OPTION_VALUE_DECAY_CAP = 0.5
86	COORDINATION_EDGE_FLOOR = 0.04
87	COORDINATION_EDGE_CEILING = 0.30
88	LOW_CONFIDENCE_PENALTY = 0.4
89	
90	
91	@dataclass(frozen=True)
92	class FrameContext:
93	    """Shared input bundle the frames operate on.
94	
95	    Frames pull only what they need. Keeping every shared input in
96	    one frozen dataclass means new frames don't need to grow new
97	    arguments — they just consult more fields. ``metrics`` is the
98	    canonical metric map from :mod:`noosphere.forecasts.decision_metrics`;
99	    keys are the metric names listed in §3 of the architecture doc.
100	    """
101	
102	    side: str | None
103	    metrics: Mapping[str, "MetricView"]
104	    transfer_best_stance: str | None = None
105	    transfer_best_principle_id: str | None = None
106	    transfer_recommendation_count: int = 0
107	    position_state: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
108	    incentive_signals: tuple[str, ...] = ()
109	    coordination_signals: tuple[str, ...] = ()
110	    reflexive_signals: tuple[str, ...] = ()
111	    market_closed: bool = False
112	    extras: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
113	
114	    def metric(self, name: str) -> "MetricView | None":
115	        return self.metrics.get(name)
116	
117	    def metric_value(self, name: str, default: float = 0.0) -> float:
118	        m = self.metrics.get(name)
119	        if m is None:
120	            return default
121	        return float(m.value)
122	
123	    def metric_low_conf(self, name: str) -> bool:
124	        m = self.metrics.get(name)
125	        if m is None:
126	            return True
127	        return bool(m.low_confidence)
128	
129	
130	@dataclass(frozen=True)
131	class MetricView:
132	    """Minimal view of a decision metric that frames need.
133	
134	    Avoids importing ``DecisionMetric`` (defined in
135	    :mod:`noosphere.forecasts.decision_metrics`) so the decisions
136	    package can be used standalone — e.g. in tests that construct
137	    contexts without spinning up the full forecast pipeline. The
138	    :func:`from_decision_metrics` helper builds a context from the
139	    real metric objects.
140	    """
141	
142	    name: str
143	    value: float
144	    low_confidence: bool = False
145	
146	
147	@dataclass(frozen=True)
148	class FrameResult:
149	    """Output of a single frame."""
150	
151	    name: str
152	    verdict: FrameVerdict
153	    assumptions_stable: bool
154	    confidence: float
155	    side_preference: str | None
156	    metrics_consulted: tuple[str, ...]
157	    reasons: tuple[str, ...]
158	    failure_modes: tuple[str, ...]
159	    detail: str = ""
160	
161	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
162	        return {
163	            "name": self.name,
164	            "verdict": self.verdict.value,
165	            "assumptions_stable": bool(self.assumptions_stable),
166	            "confidence": round(float(self.confidence), 6),
167	            "side_preference": self.side_preference,
168	            "metrics_consulted": list(self.metrics_consulted),
169	            "reasons": list(self.reasons),
170	            "failure_modes": list(self.failure_modes),
171	            "detail": self.detail,
172	        }
173	
174	
175	Frame = Callable[[FrameContext], FrameResult]
176	
177	
178	def _clamp(value: float, low: float = 0.0, high: float = 1.0) -> float:
179	    return max(low, min(high, value))
180	
181	
182	def _stake_signal_from_metrics(ctx: FrameContext) -> tuple[float, bool]:
183	    """How confident should a frame be by default? Driven by
184	    ``low_confidence`` flags on the metrics it consulted.
185	    """
186	    edge = ctx.metric("market_mispricing_edge")
187	    confidence = ctx.metric("calibration_adjusted_confidence")
188	    abs_edge = abs(edge.value) if edge and not edge.low_confidence else 0.0
189	    base = confidence.value if confidence and not confidence.low_confidence else 0.4
190	    low_conf_present = any(m.low_confidence for m in ctx.metrics.values())
191	    scalar = _clamp((abs_edge + base) / 2.0)
192	    if low_conf_present:
193	        scalar *= 1.0 - LOW_CONFIDENCE_PENALTY
194	    return scalar, low_conf_present
195	
196	
197	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
198	# Frame: contradiction / adversarial pressure
199	#
200	# Assumption: high contradiction in the supporting citations means the
201	# underlying claim is actively disputed within our own evidence set.
202	# That's not a confidence wobble; it's a categorical reason to refuse
203	# the trade. Lower contradiction widens to WATCH rather than SUPPORT,
204	# because *no* contradiction in a sample of three citations is weak
205	# evidence of robustness.
206	
207	
208	def contradiction_frame(ctx: FrameContext) -> FrameResult:
209	    contradiction = ctx.metric("contradiction_pressure")
210	    failure_modes = (
211	        "we suppress an adversarial citation by accident",
212	        "contradiction metric understates the conflict because the contrary citation is short",
213	    )
214	    metrics_consulted = ("contradiction_pressure",)
215	    if contradiction is None or contradiction.low_confidence:
216	        return FrameResult(
217	            name="contradiction",
218	            verdict=FrameVerdict.ABSTAIN,
219	            assumptions_stable=False,
220	            confidence=0.0,
221	            side_preference=None,
222	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
223	            reasons=("contradiction_pressure is missing or low_confidence",),
224	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
225	            detail="no contradiction signal to evaluate",
226	        )
227	    value = contradiction.value
228	    if value >= CONTRADICTION_HARD_STOP:
229	        return FrameResult(
230	            name="contradiction",
231	            verdict=FrameVerdict.HARD_STOP,
232	            assumptions_stable=True,
233	            confidence=_clamp(value),
234	            side_preference=None,
235	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
236	            reasons=(
237	                f"contradiction_pressure={value:.3f} ≥ hard-stop τ={CONTRADICTION_HARD_STOP}",
238	            ),
239	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
240	            detail="categorical disagreement among the load-bearing citations",
241	        )
242	    if value >= CONTRADICTION_WATCH:
243	        return FrameResult(
244	            name="contradiction",
245	            verdict=FrameVerdict.WATCH,
246	            assumptions_stable=True,
247	            confidence=_clamp(1.0 - value),
248	            side_preference=ctx.side,
249	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
250	            reasons=(
251	                f"contradiction_pressure={value:.3f} above WATCH floor {CONTRADICTION_WATCH}",
252	            ),
253	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
254	            detail="mild conflict — surface but do not stake",
255	        )
256	    return FrameResult(
257	        name="contradiction",
258	        verdict=FrameVerdict.SUPPORT,
259	        assumptions_stable=True,
260	        confidence=_clamp(1.0 - value),
261	        side_preference=ctx.side,
262	        metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
263	        reasons=(
264	            f"contradiction_pressure={value:.3f} below WATCH floor {CONTRADICTION_WATCH}",
265	        ),
266	        failure_modes=failure_modes,
267	        detail="evidence does not actively contradict the proposed view",
268	    )
269	
270	
271	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
272	# Frame: incentive alignment
273	#
274	# Caller supplies named incentive signals (e.g. "issuer_short_position",
275	# "operator_compensated_on_volume"). Any such signal whose token starts
276	# with "conflict:" or whose name is in the curated red list is a
277	# hard-stop. Otherwise the frame falls back to contradiction_pressure
278	# as a soft proxy for "does the rest of the evidence disagree about
279	# who benefits?".
280	
281	
282	_INCENTIVE_REDLIST = frozenset(
283	    {
284	        "issuer_conflict",
285	        "operator_conflict",
286	        "insider_unhedged",
287	        "principal_self_dealing",
288	        "sponsor_payoff_aligned_with_yes",
289	        "sponsor_payoff_aligned_with_no",
290	    }
291	)
292	
293	
294	def incentive_alignment_frame(ctx: FrameContext) -> FrameResult:
295	    failure_modes = (
296	        "a curated red-list signal is missing for a new conflict pattern",
297	        "we treat a tag we don't recognize as benign",
298	    )
299	    metrics_consulted = ("contradiction_pressure",)
300	    signals = tuple(s for s in ctx.incentive_signals if s)
301	    hard_signals = [s for s in signals if s in _INCENTIVE_REDLIST or s.startswith("conflict:")]
302	    if hard_signals:
303	        return FrameResult(
304	            name="incentive_alignment",
305	            verdict=FrameVerdict.HARD_STOP,
306	            assumptions_stable=True,
307	            confidence=1.0,
308	            side_preference=None,
309	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
310	            reasons=tuple(f"incentive red-list: {s}" for s in hard_signals),
311	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
312	            detail="at least one known conflict-of-interest signal is present",
313	        )
314	    contradiction = ctx.metric("contradiction_pressure")
315	    cv = contradiction.value if contradiction and not contradiction.low_confidence else 0.0
316	    if cv >= CONTRADICTION_HARD_STOP:
317	        return FrameResult(
318	            name="incentive_alignment",
319	            verdict=FrameVerdict.WATCH,
320	            assumptions_stable=True,
321	            confidence=_clamp(1.0 - cv),
322	            side_preference=ctx.side,
323	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
324	            reasons=(
325	                f"no explicit conflict signal, but contradiction_pressure={cv:.3f} suggests disputed incentives",
326	            ),
327	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
328	            detail="incentive frame deferring to contradiction frame on disputed evidence",
329	        )
330	    return FrameResult(
331	        name="incentive_alignment",
332	        verdict=FrameVerdict.SUPPORT,
333	        assumptions_stable=True,
334	        confidence=_clamp(1.0 - cv),
335	        side_preference=ctx.side,
336	        metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
337	        reasons=("no red-list incentive signal; evidence is not actively disputed",),
338	        failure_modes=failure_modes,
339	        detail="no known conflict pattern detected",
340	    )
341	
342	
343	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
344	# Frame: coordination / equilibrium
345	#
346	# A market price is itself an equilibrium summary of other agents'
347	# beliefs. The coordination frame asks: when our edge is *very* small
348	# (price ≈ our estimate), the equilibrium already reflects what we
349	# know — WATCH not SUPPORT. When the edge is implausibly large, it
350	# may reflect a coordination failure (everyone else has private
351	# information we don't) — WATCH again. The sweet spot is moderate edge.
352	
353	
354	def coordination_equilibrium_frame(ctx: FrameContext) -> FrameResult:
355	    failure_modes = (
356	        "the implied equilibrium reflects a thin book, not consensus",
357	        "we are anchoring on stale order-book prices",
358	    )
359	    metrics_consulted = (
360	        "market_mispricing_edge",
361	        "liquidity_cost_feasibility",
362	    )
363	    edge = ctx.metric("market_mispricing_edge")
364	    liquidity = ctx.metric("liquidity_cost_feasibility")
365	    if edge is None or edge.low_confidence:
366	        return FrameResult(
367	            name="coordination_equilibrium",
368	            verdict=FrameVerdict.ABSTAIN,
369	            assumptions_stable=False,
370	            confidence=0.0,
371	            side_preference=None,
372	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
373	            reasons=("market_mispricing_edge missing — no equilibrium signal",),
374	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
375	            detail="cannot evaluate coordination without a comparable price",
376	        )
377	    abs_edge = abs(edge.value)
378	    signals = tuple(s for s in ctx.coordination_signals if s)
379	    thin_book = "thin_book" in signals or (liquidity is not None and liquidity.value <= 0.2 and not liquidity.low_confidence)
380	    if abs_edge < COORDINATION_EDGE_FLOOR:
381	        return FrameResult(
382	            name="coordination_equilibrium",
383	            verdict=FrameVerdict.WATCH,
384	            assumptions_stable=True,
385	            confidence=_clamp(0.5 - abs_edge),
386	            side_preference=ctx.side,
387	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
388	            reasons=(
389	                f"|edge|={abs_edge:.3f} < equilibrium floor {COORDINATION_EDGE_FLOOR};"
390	                " market already prices our view",
391	            ),
392	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
393	            detail="price ≈ our estimate — no coordination advantage",
394	        )
395	    if abs_edge > COORDINATION_EDGE_CEILING or thin_book:
396	        return FrameResult(
397	            name="coordination_equilibrium",
398	            verdict=FrameVerdict.WATCH,
399	            assumptions_stable=not thin_book,
400	            confidence=_clamp(0.5 - (abs_edge - COORDINATION_EDGE_CEILING)),
401	            side_preference=ctx.side,
402	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
403	            reasons=(
404	                (f"|edge|={abs_edge:.3f} > coordination ceiling {COORDINATION_EDGE_CEILING};"
405	                 " implausibly large edge suggests we are missing information")
406	                if abs_edge > COORDINATION_EDGE_CEILING
407	                else "liquidity flagged as thin → no informative equilibrium",
408	            ),
409	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
410	            detail="edge is implausibly large or book is thin",
411	        )
412	    return FrameResult(
413	        name="coordination_equilibrium",
414	        verdict=FrameVerdict.SUPPORT,
415	        assumptions_stable=True,
416	        confidence=_clamp(abs_edge),
417	        side_preference=ctx.side,
418	        metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
419	        reasons=(
420	            f"|edge|={abs_edge:.3f} in coordination window "
421	            f"[{COORDINATION_EDGE_FLOOR}, {COORDINATION_EDGE_CEILING}]",
422	        ),
423	        failure_modes=failure_modes,
424	        detail="market disagrees with us within a plausible band",
425	    )
426	
427	
428	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
429	# Frame: principal-agent
430	#
431	# When we already hold a position whose underlying principle was
432	# revoked, exit. When our open side now disagrees with the edge, the
433	# agent (the position) is no longer serving the principal (the thesis);
434	# reduce. Otherwise the frame stays silent (SUPPORT) — it has nothing
435	# to say about a fresh trade.
436	
437	
438	def principal_agent_frame(ctx: FrameContext) -> FrameResult:
439	    failure_modes = (
440	        "we treat a revoked-principle signal as benign on a partial position",
441	        "open_side is missing or inconsistent with the actual book",
442	    )
443	    metrics_consulted = ("market_mispricing_edge",)
444	    state = ctx.position_state or {}
445	    revoked = bool(state.get("principles_revoked", False))
446	    open_side = str(state.get("open_side", "") or "").upper() or None
447	    if revoked:
448	        return FrameResult(
449	            name="principal_agent",
450	            verdict=FrameVerdict.EXIT,
451	            assumptions_stable=True,
452	            confidence=1.0,
453	            side_preference=None,
454	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
455	            reasons=("a principle backing the open position was revoked → exit",),
456	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
457	            detail="principal (thesis) no longer endorses the agent (position)",
458	        )
459	    edge = ctx.metric("market_mispricing_edge")
460	    edge_low = edge is None or edge.low_confidence
461	    if open_side and ctx.side and open_side != ctx.side and not edge_low:
462	        return FrameResult(
463	            name="principal_agent",
464	            verdict=FrameVerdict.REDUCE,
465	            assumptions_stable=True,
466	            confidence=_clamp(abs(edge.value) if edge else 0.0),
467	            side_preference=ctx.side,
468	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
469	            reasons=(
470	                f"open position side {open_side} disagrees with current edge side {ctx.side}",
471	            ),
472	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
473	            detail="position no longer expresses the live thesis",
474	        )
475	    return FrameResult(
476	        name="principal_agent",
477	        verdict=FrameVerdict.SUPPORT,
478	        assumptions_stable=True,
479	        confidence=0.5 if not open_side else 0.75,
480	        side_preference=ctx.side,
481	        metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
482	        reasons=("no principal-agent conflict detected for this position state",),
483	        failure_modes=failure_modes,
484	        detail="no open conflict between thesis and position",
485	    )
486	
487	
488	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
489	# Frame: reflexivity / second-order effects
490	#
491	# Reflexivity here: large naive edges that depend on the market not
492	# moving toward our estimate. When temporal decay is also high, the
493	# market is likely to adjust before resolution — the "edge" is partly
494	# illusory. The frame downgrades SUPPORT → WATCH in that regime.
495	
496	
497	def reflexivity_frame(ctx: FrameContext) -> FrameResult:
498	    failure_modes = (
499	        "we mis-estimate decay when the question is binary and resolves discretely",
500	        "second-order effects compound faster than we modeled",
501	    )
502	    metrics_consulted = (
503	        "market_mispricing_edge",
504	        "temporal_decay_pressure",
505	    )
506	    edge = ctx.metric("market_mispricing_edge")
507	    decay = ctx.metric("temporal_decay_pressure")
508	    if edge is None or edge.low_confidence:
509	        return FrameResult(
510	            name="reflexivity",
511	            verdict=FrameVerdict.ABSTAIN,
512	            assumptions_stable=False,
513	            confidence=0.0,
514	            side_preference=None,
515	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
516	            reasons=("no edge to reason about reflexively",),
517	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
518	            detail="missing edge metric",
519	        )
520	    abs_edge = abs(edge.value)
521	    decay_value = decay.value if decay and not decay.low_confidence else 0.5
522	    explicit = bool(ctx.reflexive_signals)
523	    if explicit:
524	        reasons = (
525	            "caller flagged explicit reflexive signals: " + ", ".join(ctx.reflexive_signals),
526	        )
527	        return FrameResult(
528	            name="reflexivity",
529	            verdict=FrameVerdict.WATCH,
530	            assumptions_stable=False,
531	            confidence=0.4,
532	            side_preference=ctx.side,
533	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
534	            reasons=reasons,
535	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
536	            detail="reflexive feedback explicitly named — naive edge is suspect",
537	        )
538	    if abs_edge >= REFLEXIVITY_EDGE_DOWNGRADE and decay_value >= REFLEXIVITY_DECAY_WATCH:
539	        return FrameResult(
540	            name="reflexivity",
541	            verdict=FrameVerdict.WATCH,
542	            assumptions_stable=True,
543	            confidence=_clamp(1.0 - decay_value),
544	            side_preference=ctx.side,
545	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
546	            reasons=(
547	                f"|edge|={abs_edge:.3f} large enough to provoke reaction"
548	                f" and temporal_decay_pressure={decay_value:.3f} ≥ {REFLEXIVITY_DECAY_WATCH}",
549	            ),
550	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
551	            detail="market is likely to move toward our estimate before resolution",
552	        )
553	    return FrameResult(
554	        name="reflexivity",
555	        verdict=FrameVerdict.SUPPORT,
556	        assumptions_stable=True,
557	        confidence=_clamp(abs_edge - decay_value * 0.25, 0.0, 1.0),
558	        side_preference=ctx.side,
559	        metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
560	        reasons=(
561	            f"|edge|={abs_edge:.3f} and decay={decay_value:.3f} below reflexivity-watch thresholds",
562	        ),
563	        failure_modes=failure_modes,
564	        detail="no obvious reflexive feedback expected",
565	    )
566	
567	
568	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
569	# Frame: option value / asymmetry
570	#
571	# Acting now vs. waiting: when our calibration-adjusted confidence is
572	# weak but the edge is real, the option value of waiting (more data,
573	# resolved questions, fewer unknowns) is often positive — WATCH.
574	# When confidence is strong *and* time-to-resolution is short
575	# (high decay), waiting is no longer free — SUPPORT.
576	
577	
578	def option_value_frame(ctx: FrameContext) -> FrameResult:
579	    failure_modes = (
580	        "we discount option value when the underlying volatility is high",
581	        "we overpay for optionality the market also prices in",
582	    )
583	    metrics_consulted = (
584	        "calibration_adjusted_confidence",
585	        "temporal_decay_pressure",
586	        "market_mispricing_edge",
587	    )
588	    edge = ctx.metric("market_mispricing_edge")
589	    confidence = ctx.metric("calibration_adjusted_confidence")
590	    decay = ctx.metric("temporal_decay_pressure")
591	    if edge is None or edge.low_confidence:
592	        return FrameResult(
593	            name="option_value",
594	            verdict=FrameVerdict.ABSTAIN,
595	            assumptions_stable=False,
596	            confidence=0.0,
597	            side_preference=None,
598	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
599	            reasons=("no edge — option value of waiting cannot be compared",),
600	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
601	            detail="missing edge",
602	        )
603	    conf_value = confidence.value if confidence and not confidence.low_confidence else 0.0
604	    decay_value = decay.value if decay and not decay.low_confidence else 0.0
605	    if conf_value < OPTION_VALUE_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR and decay_value < OPTION_VALUE_DECAY_CAP:
606	        return FrameResult(
607	            name="option_value",
608	            verdict=FrameVerdict.WATCH,
609	            assumptions_stable=confidence is not None and not confidence.low_confidence,
610	            confidence=_clamp(OPTION_VALUE_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR - conf_value),
611	            side_preference=ctx.side,
612	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
613	            reasons=(
614	                f"confidence={conf_value:.3f} < {OPTION_VALUE_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR}"
615	                f" and decay={decay_value:.3f} leaves time to wait",
616	            ),
617	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
618	            detail="option value of waiting > option value of acting",
619	        )
620	    return FrameResult(
621	        name="option_value",
622	        verdict=FrameVerdict.SUPPORT,
623	        assumptions_stable=confidence is not None and not confidence.low_confidence,
624	        confidence=_clamp(conf_value),
625	        side_preference=ctx.side,
626	        metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
627	        reasons=(
628	            f"confidence={conf_value:.3f} ≥ floor or decay={decay_value:.3f} ≥ cap — acting now",
629	        ),
630	        failure_modes=failure_modes,
631	        detail="waiting is no longer cheap",
632	    )
633	
634	
635	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
636	# Frame: empirical transfer
637	#
638	# Maps the analogical-transfer best stance into a frame verdict.
639	# DOES_NOT_APPLY is a hard-stop only if the transfer report had
640	# enough recommendations to be informative; a thin report degrades
641	# to ABSTAIN (unstable assumption) instead.
642	
643	
644	def empirical_transfer_frame(ctx: FrameContext) -> FrameResult:
645	    failure_modes = (
646	        "the closest empirical case is structurally similar but causally different",
647	        "we have too few cases to draw a stance",
648	    )
649	    metrics_consulted = ()
650	    stance = (ctx.transfer_best_stance or "").upper()
651	    n = ctx.transfer_recommendation_count
652	    principle_id = ctx.transfer_best_principle_id
653	    if not stance:
654	        return FrameResult(
655	            name="empirical_transfer",
656	            verdict=FrameVerdict.ABSTAIN,
657	            assumptions_stable=False,
658	            confidence=0.0,
659	            side_preference=None,
660	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
661	            reasons=("no analogical transfer report attached",),
662	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
663	            detail="empirical transfer unavailable",
664	        )
665	    if stance == "DOES_NOT_APPLY":
666	        if n >= 2:
667	            return FrameResult(
668	                name="empirical_transfer",
669	                verdict=FrameVerdict.HARD_STOP,
670	                assumptions_stable=True,
671	                confidence=1.0,
672	                side_preference=None,
673	                metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
674	                reasons=(
675	                    f"transfer best_stance=DOES_NOT_APPLY across {n} recommendations"
676	                    + (f" (principle={principle_id})" if principle_id else ""),
677	                ),
678	                failure_modes=failure_modes,
679	                detail="empirically analogous cases reject this thesis",
680	            )
681	        return FrameResult(
682	            name="empirical_transfer",
683	            verdict=FrameVerdict.ABSTAIN,
684	            assumptions_stable=False,
685	            confidence=0.5,
686	            side_preference=None,
687	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
688	            reasons=(
689	                f"transfer best_stance=DOES_NOT_APPLY but only {n} recommendations — insufficient base",
690	            ),
691	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
692	            detail="single negative transfer; abstain rather than veto",
693	        )
694	    if stance == "APPLIES":
695	        return FrameResult(
696	            name="empirical_transfer",
697	            verdict=FrameVerdict.SUPPORT,
698	            assumptions_stable=True,
699	            confidence=_clamp(min(1.0, 0.4 + 0.1 * n)),
700	            side_preference=ctx.side,
701	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
702	            reasons=(
703	                f"transfer best_stance=APPLIES across {n} recommendations"
704	                + (f" (principle={principle_id})" if principle_id else ""),
705	            ),
706	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
707	            detail="prior cases support the thesis",
708	        )
709	    if stance == "WATCH":
710	        return FrameResult(
711	            name="empirical_transfer",
712	            verdict=FrameVerdict.WATCH,
713	            assumptions_stable=True,
714	            confidence=0.4,
715	            side_preference=ctx.side,
716	            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
717	            reasons=(
718	                f"transfer best_stance=WATCH across {n} recommendations",
719	            ),
720	            failure_modes=failure_modes,
721	            detail="prior cases ambivalent",
722	        )
723	    return FrameResult(
724	        name="empirical_transfer",
725	        verdict=FrameVerdict.ABSTAIN,
726	        assumptions_stable=False,
727	        confidence=0.2,
728	        side_preference=None,
729	        metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
730	        reasons=(f"transfer best_stance={stance} — unstable for action",),
731	        failure_modes=failure_modes,
732	        detail="empirical transfer is non-committal",
733	    )
734	
735	
736	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
737	# Frame registry / runner
738	
739	
740	DEFAULT_FRAMES: tuple[Frame, ...] = (
741	    incentive_alignment_frame,
742	    coordination_equilibrium_frame,
743	    principal_agent_frame,
744	    reflexivity_frame,
745	    option_value_frame,
746	    contradiction_frame,
747	    empirical_transfer_frame,
748	)
749	
750	
751	def run_frames(
752	    ctx: FrameContext,
753	    frames: Sequence[Frame] = DEFAULT_FRAMES,
754	) -> tuple[FrameResult, ...]:
755	    """Evaluate every frame against the context. Order is preserved."""
756	    return tuple(frame(ctx) for frame in frames)
757	
758	
759	def context_from_decision_metrics(
760	    *,
761	    side: str | None,
762	    metrics: Sequence[Any],
763	    transfer_report: Any | None = None,
764	    position_state: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
765	    incentive_signals: Sequence[str] = (),
766	    coordination_signals: Sequence[str] = (),
767	    reflexive_signals: Sequence[str] = (),
768	    market_closed: bool = False,
769	) -> FrameContext:
770	    """Build a :class:`FrameContext` from
771	    :class:`noosphere.forecasts.decision_metrics.DecisionMetric` rows.
772	
773	    Kept in this module (not in ``decision_metrics``) so the decisions
774	    package depends on forecasts only via duck typing — same shape,
775	    no import cycle.
776	    """
777	    metric_views: dict[str, MetricView] = {}
778	    for metric in metrics:
779	        metric_views[str(metric.name)] = MetricView(
780	            name=str(metric.name),
781	            value=float(metric.value),
782	            low_confidence=bool(getattr(metric, "low_confidence", False)),
783	        )
784	    stance = None
785	    principle_id = None
786	    rec_count = 0
787	    if transfer_report is not None:
788	        stance = getattr(transfer_report, "best_stance", None) or None
789	        principle_id = getattr(transfer_report, "best_principle_id", None)
790	        recs = getattr(transfer_report, "recommendations", ()) or ()
791	        rec_count = len(recs)
792	    return FrameContext(
793	        side=side,
794	        metrics=metric_views,
795	        transfer_best_stance=str(stance) if stance else None,
796	        transfer_best_principle_id=str(principle_id) if principle_id else None,
797	        transfer_recommendation_count=rec_count,
798	        position_state=dict(position_state or {}),
799	        incentive_signals=tuple(incentive_signals),
800	        coordination_signals=tuple(coordination_signals),
801	        reflexive_signals=tuple(reflexive_signals),
802	        market_closed=bool(market_closed),
803	    )
804	
805	
806	__all__ = [
807	    "CONTRADICTION_HARD_STOP",
808	    "CONTRADICTION_WATCH",
809	    "COORDINATION_EDGE_CEILING",
810	    "COORDINATION_EDGE_FLOOR",
811	    "DEFAULT_FRAMES",
812	    "FRAMES_VERSION",
813	    "Frame",
814	    "FrameContext",
815	    "FrameResult",
816	    "FrameVerdict",
817	    "MetricView",
818	    "OPTION_VALUE_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR",
819	    "OPTION_VALUE_DECAY_CAP",
820	    "REFLEXIVITY_DECAY_WATCH",
821	    "REFLEXIVITY_EDGE_DOWNGRADE",
822	    "context_from_decision_metrics",
823	    "contradiction_frame",
824	    "coordination_equilibrium_frame",
825	    "empirical_transfer_frame",
826	    "incentive_alignment_frame",
827	    "option_value_frame",
828	    "principal_agent_frame",
829	    "reflexivity_frame",
830	    "run_frames",
831	    "verdict_severity",
832	]
833

"""Decision frames — orthogonal lenses on a candidate trade.

Each frame is a small, pure function over :class:`FrameContext` that
returns a :class:`FrameResult`. A frame describes:

- the *name* of the lens it applies (e.g. "incentive_alignment");
- the *inputs* it consumes (which metrics, which context fields);
- the *assumptions* the lens rests on (and whether they look stable
  in this context);
- a verdict (:class:`FrameVerdict`) — the lens's standalone vote;
- the *failure modes* the frame is on the lookout for (purely
  descriptive — used in the trace).

The frame layer deliberately does not synthesize. It only emits
results. :func:`noosphere.decisions.synthesis.synthesize` is the only
place where multiple frames are combined into a single action — and
that combiner is itself a deterministic rule, not a model.

Why split this up at all? The architecture contract in
``docs/architecture/Algorithmized_Decision_Making.md`` (§1.2 and §2.5)
wants the trace to be *the* artifact: prose flows from the trace, not
the other way around. Multiple inspectable frames make the trace
genuinely inspectable — a reader can ask "which lenses agreed?" and
"which one blocked?" without reverse-engineering one collapsed score.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum
from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping, Sequence


FRAMES_VERSION = "decision_frames@v1"


class FrameVerdict(str, Enum):
    """A single frame's vote on the candidate trade.

    Ordering of severity (low → high) when synthesis breaks ties:

    - ``SUPPORT``  — frame is comfortable with the trade as proposed.
    - ``WATCH``    — frame sees enough signal to surface, not stake.
    - ``ABSTAIN``  — frame's assumptions don't hold in this context.
    - ``REDUCE``   — frame is calling for less exposure (existing position).
    - ``EXIT``     — frame is calling for full closure (existing position).
    - ``HEDGE``    — frame is calling for offsetting exposure.
    - ``HARD_STOP``— frame considers the trade categorically forbidden
                     here; ``HARD_STOP`` from any frame forces
                     ``ABSTAIN`` at synthesis regardless of agreement
                     elsewhere.
    """

    SUPPORT = "SUPPORT"
    WATCH = "WATCH"
    ABSTAIN = "ABSTAIN"
    REDUCE = "REDUCE"
    EXIT = "EXIT"
    HEDGE = "HEDGE"
    HARD_STOP = "HARD_STOP"


_VERDICT_RANK = {
    FrameVerdict.SUPPORT: 0,
    FrameVerdict.WATCH: 1,
    FrameVerdict.ABSTAIN: 2,
    FrameVerdict.REDUCE: 3,
    FrameVerdict.EXIT: 4,
    FrameVerdict.HEDGE: 5,
    FrameVerdict.HARD_STOP: 6,
}


def verdict_severity(verdict: FrameVerdict) -> int:
    return _VERDICT_RANK[verdict]


# Thresholds the frames consult. Held here (not env-driven) so the
# decision trace is reproducible from its inputs alone.
CONTRADICTION_HARD_STOP = 0.5
CONTRADICTION_WATCH = 0.25
REFLEXIVITY_DECAY_WATCH = 0.5
REFLEXIVITY_EDGE_DOWNGRADE = 0.15
OPTION_VALUE_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR = 0.45
OPTION_VALUE_DECAY_CAP = 0.5
COORDINATION_EDGE_FLOOR = 0.04
COORDINATION_EDGE_CEILING = 0.30
LOW_CONFIDENCE_PENALTY = 0.4


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class FrameContext:
    """Shared input bundle the frames operate on.

    Frames pull only what they need. Keeping every shared input in
    one frozen dataclass means new frames don't need to grow new
    arguments — they just consult more fields. ``metrics`` is the
    canonical metric map from :mod:`noosphere.forecasts.decision_metrics`;
    keys are the metric names listed in §3 of the architecture doc.
    """

    side: str | None
    metrics: Mapping[str, "MetricView"]
    transfer_best_stance: str | None = None
    transfer_best_principle_id: str | None = None
    transfer_recommendation_count: int = 0
    position_state: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    incentive_signals: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    coordination_signals: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    reflexive_signals: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    market_closed: bool = False
    extras: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

    def metric(self, name: str) -> "MetricView | None":
        return self.metrics.get(name)

    def metric_value(self, name: str, default: float = 0.0) -> float:
        m = self.metrics.get(name)
        if m is None:
            return default
        return float(m.value)

    def metric_low_conf(self, name: str) -> bool:
        m = self.metrics.get(name)
        if m is None:
            return True
        return bool(m.low_confidence)


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MetricView:
    """Minimal view of a decision metric that frames need.

    Avoids importing ``DecisionMetric`` (defined in
    :mod:`noosphere.forecasts.decision_metrics`) so the decisions
    package can be used standalone — e.g. in tests that construct
    contexts without spinning up the full forecast pipeline. The
    :func:`from_decision_metrics` helper builds a context from the
    real metric objects.
    """

    name: str
    value: float
    low_confidence: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class FrameResult:
    """Output of a single frame."""

    name: str
    verdict: FrameVerdict
    assumptions_stable: bool
    confidence: float
    side_preference: str | None
    metrics_consulted: tuple[str, ...]
    reasons: tuple[str, ...]
    failure_modes: tuple[str, ...]
    detail: str = ""

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "name": self.name,
            "verdict": self.verdict.value,
            "assumptions_stable": bool(self.assumptions_stable),
            "confidence": round(float(self.confidence), 6),
            "side_preference": self.side_preference,
            "metrics_consulted": list(self.metrics_consulted),
            "reasons": list(self.reasons),
            "failure_modes": list(self.failure_modes),
            "detail": self.detail,
        }


Frame = Callable[[FrameContext], FrameResult]


def _clamp(value: float, low: float = 0.0, high: float = 1.0) -> float:
    return max(low, min(high, value))


def _stake_signal_from_metrics(ctx: FrameContext) -> tuple[float, bool]:
    """How confident should a frame be by default? Driven by
    ``low_confidence`` flags on the metrics it consulted.
    """
    edge = ctx.metric("market_mispricing_edge")
    confidence = ctx.metric("calibration_adjusted_confidence")
    abs_edge = abs(edge.value) if edge and not edge.low_confidence else 0.0
    base = confidence.value if confidence and not confidence.low_confidence else 0.4
    low_conf_present = any(m.low_confidence for m in ctx.metrics.values())
    scalar = _clamp((abs_edge + base) / 2.0)
    if low_conf_present:
        scalar *= 1.0 - LOW_CONFIDENCE_PENALTY
    return scalar, low_conf_present


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Frame: contradiction / adversarial pressure
#
# Assumption: high contradiction in the supporting citations means the
# underlying claim is actively disputed within our own evidence set.
# That's not a confidence wobble; it's a categorical reason to refuse
# the trade. Lower contradiction widens to WATCH rather than SUPPORT,
# because *no* contradiction in a sample of three citations is weak
# evidence of robustness.


def contradiction_frame(ctx: FrameContext) -> FrameResult:
    contradiction = ctx.metric("contradiction_pressure")
    failure_modes = (
        "we suppress an adversarial citation by accident",
        "contradiction metric understates the conflict because the contrary citation is short",
    )
    metrics_consulted = ("contradiction_pressure",)
    if contradiction is None or contradiction.low_confidence:
        return FrameResult(
            name="contradiction",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.ABSTAIN,
            assumptions_stable=False,
            confidence=0.0,
            side_preference=None,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=("contradiction_pressure is missing or low_confidence",),
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="no contradiction signal to evaluate",
        )
    value = contradiction.value
    if value >= CONTRADICTION_HARD_STOP:
        return FrameResult(
            name="contradiction",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.HARD_STOP,
            assumptions_stable=True,
            confidence=_clamp(value),
            side_preference=None,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=(
                f"contradiction_pressure={value:.3f} ≥ hard-stop τ={CONTRADICTION_HARD_STOP}",
            ),
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="categorical disagreement among the load-bearing citations",
        )
    if value >= CONTRADICTION_WATCH:
        return FrameResult(
            name="contradiction",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.WATCH,
            assumptions_stable=True,
            confidence=_clamp(1.0 - value),
            side_preference=ctx.side,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=(
                f"contradiction_pressure={value:.3f} above WATCH floor {CONTRADICTION_WATCH}",
            ),
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="mild conflict — surface but do not stake",
        )
    return FrameResult(
        name="contradiction",
        verdict=FrameVerdict.SUPPORT,
        assumptions_stable=True,
        confidence=_clamp(1.0 - value),
        side_preference=ctx.side,
        metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
        reasons=(
            f"contradiction_pressure={value:.3f} below WATCH floor {CONTRADICTION_WATCH}",
        ),
        failure_modes=failure_modes,
        detail="evidence does not actively contradict the proposed view",
    )


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Frame: incentive alignment
#
# Caller supplies named incentive signals (e.g. "issuer_short_position",
# "operator_compensated_on_volume"). Any such signal whose token starts
# with "conflict:" or whose name is in the curated red list is a
# hard-stop. Otherwise the frame falls back to contradiction_pressure
# as a soft proxy for "does the rest of the evidence disagree about
# who benefits?".


_INCENTIVE_REDLIST = frozenset(
    {
        "issuer_conflict",
        "operator_conflict",
        "insider_unhedged",
        "principal_self_dealing",
        "sponsor_payoff_aligned_with_yes",
        "sponsor_payoff_aligned_with_no",
    }
)


def incentive_alignment_frame(ctx: FrameContext) -> FrameResult:
    failure_modes = (
        "a curated red-list signal is missing for a new conflict pattern",
        "we treat a tag we don't recognize as benign",
    )
    metrics_consulted = ("contradiction_pressure",)
    signals = tuple(s for s in ctx.incentive_signals if s)
    hard_signals = [s for s in signals if s in _INCENTIVE_REDLIST or s.startswith("conflict:")]
    if hard_signals:
        return FrameResult(
            name="incentive_alignment",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.HARD_STOP,
            assumptions_stable=True,
            confidence=1.0,
            side_preference=None,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=tuple(f"incentive red-list: {s}" for s in hard_signals),
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="at least one known conflict-of-interest signal is present",
        )
    contradiction = ctx.metric("contradiction_pressure")
    cv = contradiction.value if contradiction and not contradiction.low_confidence else 0.0
    if cv >= CONTRADICTION_HARD_STOP:
        return FrameResult(
            name="incentive_alignment",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.WATCH,
            assumptions_stable=True,
            confidence=_clamp(1.0 - cv),
            side_preference=ctx.side,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=(
                f"no explicit conflict signal, but contradiction_pressure={cv:.3f} suggests disputed incentives",
            ),
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="incentive frame deferring to contradiction frame on disputed evidence",
        )
    return FrameResult(
        name="incentive_alignment",
        verdict=FrameVerdict.SUPPORT,
        assumptions_stable=True,
        confidence=_clamp(1.0 - cv),
        side_preference=ctx.side,
        metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
        reasons=("no red-list incentive signal; evidence is not actively disputed",),
        failure_modes=failure_modes,
        detail="no known conflict pattern detected",
    )


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Frame: coordination / equilibrium
#
# A market price is itself an equilibrium summary of other agents'
# beliefs. The coordination frame asks: when our edge is *very* small
# (price ≈ our estimate), the equilibrium already reflects what we
# know — WATCH not SUPPORT. When the edge is implausibly large, it
# may reflect a coordination failure (everyone else has private
# information we don't) — WATCH again. The sweet spot is moderate edge.


def coordination_equilibrium_frame(ctx: FrameContext) -> FrameResult:
    failure_modes = (
        "the implied equilibrium reflects a thin book, not consensus",
        "we are anchoring on stale order-book prices",
    )
    metrics_consulted = (
        "market_mispricing_edge",
        "liquidity_cost_feasibility",
    )
    edge = ctx.metric("market_mispricing_edge")
    liquidity = ctx.metric("liquidity_cost_feasibility")
    if edge is None or edge.low_confidence:
        return FrameResult(
            name="coordination_equilibrium",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.ABSTAIN,
            assumptions_stable=False,
            confidence=0.0,
            side_preference=None,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=("market_mispricing_edge missing — no equilibrium signal",),
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="cannot evaluate coordination without a comparable price",
        )
    abs_edge = abs(edge.value)
    signals = tuple(s for s in ctx.coordination_signals if s)
    thin_book = "thin_book" in signals or (liquidity is not None and liquidity.value <= 0.2 and not liquidity.low_confidence)
    if abs_edge < COORDINATION_EDGE_FLOOR:
        return FrameResult(
            name="coordination_equilibrium",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.WATCH,
            assumptions_stable=True,
            confidence=_clamp(0.5 - abs_edge),
            side_preference=ctx.side,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=(
                f"|edge|={abs_edge:.3f} < equilibrium floor {COORDINATION_EDGE_FLOOR};"
                " market already prices our view",
            ),
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="price ≈ our estimate — no coordination advantage",
        )
    if abs_edge > COORDINATION_EDGE_CEILING or thin_book:
        return FrameResult(
            name="coordination_equilibrium",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.WATCH,
            assumptions_stable=not thin_book,
            confidence=_clamp(0.5 - (abs_edge - COORDINATION_EDGE_CEILING)),
            side_preference=ctx.side,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=(
                (f"|edge|={abs_edge:.3f} > coordination ceiling {COORDINATION_EDGE_CEILING};"
                 " implausibly large edge suggests we are missing information")
                if abs_edge > COORDINATION_EDGE_CEILING
                else "liquidity flagged as thin → no informative equilibrium",
            ),
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="edge is implausibly large or book is thin",
        )
    return FrameResult(
        name="coordination_equilibrium",
        verdict=FrameVerdict.SUPPORT,
        assumptions_stable=True,
        confidence=_clamp(abs_edge),
        side_preference=ctx.side,
        metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
        reasons=(
            f"|edge|={abs_edge:.3f} in coordination window "
            f"[{COORDINATION_EDGE_FLOOR}, {COORDINATION_EDGE_CEILING}]",
        ),
        failure_modes=failure_modes,
        detail="market disagrees with us within a plausible band",
    )


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Frame: principal-agent
#
# When we already hold a position whose underlying principle was
# revoked, exit. When our open side now disagrees with the edge, the
# agent (the position) is no longer serving the principal (the thesis);
# reduce. Otherwise the frame stays silent (SUPPORT) — it has nothing
# to say about a fresh trade.


def principal_agent_frame(ctx: FrameContext) -> FrameResult:
    failure_modes = (
        "we treat a revoked-principle signal as benign on a partial position",
        "open_side is missing or inconsistent with the actual book",
    )
    metrics_consulted = ("market_mispricing_edge",)
    state = ctx.position_state or {}
    revoked = bool(state.get("principles_revoked", False))
    open_side = str(state.get("open_side", "") or "").upper() or None
    if revoked:
        return FrameResult(
            name="principal_agent",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.EXIT,
            assumptions_stable=True,
            confidence=1.0,
            side_preference=None,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=("a principle backing the open position was revoked → exit",),
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="principal (thesis) no longer endorses the agent (position)",
        )
    edge = ctx.metric("market_mispricing_edge")
    edge_low = edge is None or edge.low_confidence
    if open_side and ctx.side and open_side != ctx.side and not edge_low:
        return FrameResult(
            name="principal_agent",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.REDUCE,
            assumptions_stable=True,
            confidence=_clamp(abs(edge.value) if edge else 0.0),
            side_preference=ctx.side,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=(
                f"open position side {open_side} disagrees with current edge side {ctx.side}",
            ),
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="position no longer expresses the live thesis",
        )
    return FrameResult(
        name="principal_agent",
        verdict=FrameVerdict.SUPPORT,
        assumptions_stable=True,
        confidence=0.5 if not open_side else 0.75,
        side_preference=ctx.side,
        metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
        reasons=("no principal-agent conflict detected for this position state",),
        failure_modes=failure_modes,
        detail="no open conflict between thesis and position",
    )


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Frame: reflexivity / second-order effects
#
# Reflexivity here: large naive edges that depend on the market not
# moving toward our estimate. When temporal decay is also high, the
# market is likely to adjust before resolution — the "edge" is partly
# illusory. The frame downgrades SUPPORT → WATCH in that regime.


def reflexivity_frame(ctx: FrameContext) -> FrameResult:
    failure_modes = (
        "we mis-estimate decay when the question is binary and resolves discretely",
        "second-order effects compound faster than we modeled",
    )
    metrics_consulted = (
        "market_mispricing_edge",
        "temporal_decay_pressure",
    )
    edge = ctx.metric("market_mispricing_edge")
    decay = ctx.metric("temporal_decay_pressure")
    if edge is None or edge.low_confidence:
        return FrameResult(
            name="reflexivity",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.ABSTAIN,
            assumptions_stable=False,
            confidence=0.0,
            side_preference=None,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=("no edge to reason about reflexively",),
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="missing edge metric",
        )
    abs_edge = abs(edge.value)
    decay_value = decay.value if decay and not decay.low_confidence else 0.5
    explicit = bool(ctx.reflexive_signals)
    if explicit:
        reasons = (
            "caller flagged explicit reflexive signals: " + ", ".join(ctx.reflexive_signals),
        )
        return FrameResult(
            name="reflexivity",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.WATCH,
            assumptions_stable=False,
            confidence=0.4,
            side_preference=ctx.side,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=reasons,
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="reflexive feedback explicitly named — naive edge is suspect",
        )
    if abs_edge >= REFLEXIVITY_EDGE_DOWNGRADE and decay_value >= REFLEXIVITY_DECAY_WATCH:
        return FrameResult(
            name="reflexivity",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.WATCH,
            assumptions_stable=True,
            confidence=_clamp(1.0 - decay_value),
            side_preference=ctx.side,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=(
                f"|edge|={abs_edge:.3f} large enough to provoke reaction"
                f" and temporal_decay_pressure={decay_value:.3f} ≥ {REFLEXIVITY_DECAY_WATCH}",
            ),
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="market is likely to move toward our estimate before resolution",
        )
    return FrameResult(
        name="reflexivity",
        verdict=FrameVerdict.SUPPORT,
        assumptions_stable=True,
        confidence=_clamp(abs_edge - decay_value * 0.25, 0.0, 1.0),
        side_preference=ctx.side,
        metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
        reasons=(
            f"|edge|={abs_edge:.3f} and decay={decay_value:.3f} below reflexivity-watch thresholds",
        ),
        failure_modes=failure_modes,
        detail="no obvious reflexive feedback expected",
    )


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Frame: option value / asymmetry
#
# Acting now vs. waiting: when our calibration-adjusted confidence is
# weak but the edge is real, the option value of waiting (more data,
# resolved questions, fewer unknowns) is often positive — WATCH.
# When confidence is strong *and* time-to-resolution is short
# (high decay), waiting is no longer free — SUPPORT.


def option_value_frame(ctx: FrameContext) -> FrameResult:
    failure_modes = (
        "we discount option value when the underlying volatility is high",
        "we overpay for optionality the market also prices in",
    )
    metrics_consulted = (
        "calibration_adjusted_confidence",
        "temporal_decay_pressure",
        "market_mispricing_edge",
    )
    edge = ctx.metric("market_mispricing_edge")
    confidence = ctx.metric("calibration_adjusted_confidence")
    decay = ctx.metric("temporal_decay_pressure")
    if edge is None or edge.low_confidence:
        return FrameResult(
            name="option_value",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.ABSTAIN,
            assumptions_stable=False,
            confidence=0.0,
            side_preference=None,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=("no edge — option value of waiting cannot be compared",),
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="missing edge",
        )
    conf_value = confidence.value if confidence and not confidence.low_confidence else 0.0
    decay_value = decay.value if decay and not decay.low_confidence else 0.0
    if conf_value < OPTION_VALUE_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR and decay_value < OPTION_VALUE_DECAY_CAP:
        return FrameResult(
            name="option_value",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.WATCH,
            assumptions_stable=confidence is not None and not confidence.low_confidence,
            confidence=_clamp(OPTION_VALUE_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR - conf_value),
            side_preference=ctx.side,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=(
                f"confidence={conf_value:.3f} < {OPTION_VALUE_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR}"
                f" and decay={decay_value:.3f} leaves time to wait",
            ),
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="option value of waiting > option value of acting",
        )
    return FrameResult(
        name="option_value",
        verdict=FrameVerdict.SUPPORT,
        assumptions_stable=confidence is not None and not confidence.low_confidence,
        confidence=_clamp(conf_value),
        side_preference=ctx.side,
        metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
        reasons=(
            f"confidence={conf_value:.3f} ≥ floor or decay={decay_value:.3f} ≥ cap — acting now",
        ),
        failure_modes=failure_modes,
        detail="waiting is no longer cheap",
    )


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Frame: empirical transfer
#
# Maps the analogical-transfer best stance into a frame verdict.
# DOES_NOT_APPLY is a hard-stop only if the transfer report had
# enough recommendations to be informative; a thin report degrades
# to ABSTAIN (unstable assumption) instead.


def empirical_transfer_frame(ctx: FrameContext) -> FrameResult:
    failure_modes = (
        "the closest empirical case is structurally similar but causally different",
        "we have too few cases to draw a stance",
    )
    metrics_consulted = ()
    stance = (ctx.transfer_best_stance or "").upper()
    n = ctx.transfer_recommendation_count
    principle_id = ctx.transfer_best_principle_id
    if not stance:
        return FrameResult(
            name="empirical_transfer",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.ABSTAIN,
            assumptions_stable=False,
            confidence=0.0,
            side_preference=None,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=("no analogical transfer report attached",),
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="empirical transfer unavailable",
        )
    if stance == "DOES_NOT_APPLY":
        if n >= 2:
            return FrameResult(
                name="empirical_transfer",
                verdict=FrameVerdict.HARD_STOP,
                assumptions_stable=True,
                confidence=1.0,
                side_preference=None,
                metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
                reasons=(
                    f"transfer best_stance=DOES_NOT_APPLY across {n} recommendations"
                    + (f" (principle={principle_id})" if principle_id else ""),
                ),
                failure_modes=failure_modes,
                detail="empirically analogous cases reject this thesis",
            )
        return FrameResult(
            name="empirical_transfer",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.ABSTAIN,
            assumptions_stable=False,
            confidence=0.5,
            side_preference=None,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=(
                f"transfer best_stance=DOES_NOT_APPLY but only {n} recommendations — insufficient base",
            ),
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="single negative transfer; abstain rather than veto",
        )
    if stance == "APPLIES":
        return FrameResult(
            name="empirical_transfer",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.SUPPORT,
            assumptions_stable=True,
            confidence=_clamp(min(1.0, 0.4 + 0.1 * n)),
            side_preference=ctx.side,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=(
                f"transfer best_stance=APPLIES across {n} recommendations"
                + (f" (principle={principle_id})" if principle_id else ""),
            ),
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="prior cases support the thesis",
        )
    if stance == "WATCH":
        return FrameResult(
            name="empirical_transfer",
            verdict=FrameVerdict.WATCH,
            assumptions_stable=True,
            confidence=0.4,
            side_preference=ctx.side,
            metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
            reasons=(
                f"transfer best_stance=WATCH across {n} recommendations",
            ),
            failure_modes=failure_modes,
            detail="prior cases ambivalent",
        )
    return FrameResult(
        name="empirical_transfer",
        verdict=FrameVerdict.ABSTAIN,
        assumptions_stable=False,
        confidence=0.2,
        side_preference=None,
        metrics_consulted=metrics_consulted,
        reasons=(f"transfer best_stance={stance} — unstable for action",),
        failure_modes=failure_modes,
        detail="empirical transfer is non-committal",
    )


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Frame registry / runner


DEFAULT_FRAMES: tuple[Frame, ...] = (
    incentive_alignment_frame,
    coordination_equilibrium_frame,
    principal_agent_frame,
    reflexivity_frame,
    option_value_frame,
    contradiction_frame,
    empirical_transfer_frame,
)


def run_frames(
    ctx: FrameContext,
    frames: Sequence[Frame] = DEFAULT_FRAMES,
) -> tuple[FrameResult, ...]:
    """Evaluate every frame against the context. Order is preserved."""
    return tuple(frame(ctx) for frame in frames)


def context_from_decision_metrics(
    *,
    side: str | None,
    metrics: Sequence[Any],
    transfer_report: Any | None = None,
    position_state: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
    incentive_signals: Sequence[str] = (),
    coordination_signals: Sequence[str] = (),
    reflexive_signals: Sequence[str] = (),
    market_closed: bool = False,
) -> FrameContext:
    """Build a :class:`FrameContext` from
    :class:`noosphere.forecasts.decision_metrics.DecisionMetric` rows.

    Kept in this module (not in ``decision_metrics``) so the decisions
    package depends on forecasts only via duck typing — same shape,
    no import cycle.
    """
    metric_views: dict[str, MetricView] = {}
    for metric in metrics:
        metric_views[str(metric.name)] = MetricView(
            name=str(metric.name),
            value=float(metric.value),
            low_confidence=bool(getattr(metric, "low_confidence", False)),
        )
    stance = None
    principle_id = None
    rec_count = 0
    if transfer_report is not None:
        stance = getattr(transfer_report, "best_stance", None) or None
        principle_id = getattr(transfer_report, "best_principle_id", None)
        recs = getattr(transfer_report, "recommendations", ()) or ()
        rec_count = len(recs)
    return FrameContext(
        side=side,
        metrics=metric_views,
        transfer_best_stance=str(stance) if stance else None,
        transfer_best_principle_id=str(principle_id) if principle_id else None,
        transfer_recommendation_count=rec_count,
        position_state=dict(position_state or {}),
        incentive_signals=tuple(incentive_signals),
        coordination_signals=tuple(coordination_signals),
        reflexive_signals=tuple(reflexive_signals),
        market_closed=bool(market_closed),
    )


__all__ = [
    "CONTRADICTION_HARD_STOP",
    "CONTRADICTION_WATCH",
    "COORDINATION_EDGE_CEILING",
    "COORDINATION_EDGE_FLOOR",
    "DEFAULT_FRAMES",
    "FRAMES_VERSION",
    "Frame",

file.content:
model ForecastTrace {
  id             String   @id @default(cuid())
  predictionId   String   @unique
  marketId       String
  organizationId String
  marketTitle    String   @db.VarChar(280)
  principlesUsed Json
  modelOutput    Json
  gateResults    Json
  createdAt      DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt      DateTime @updatedAt

  prediction   ForecastPrediction @relation(fields: [predictionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  market       ForecastMarket     @relation(fields: [marketId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  organization Organization       @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)

  @@index([marketId])
  @@index([organizationId, createdAt])
}

model ForecastCitation {
  id             String               @id @default(cuid())
  predictionId   String
  sourceType     String
  sourceId       String
  quotedSpan     String               @db.Text
  supportLabel   ForecastSupportLabel
  retrievalScore Float?
  isRevoked      Boolean              @default(false)
  revokedReason  String?
  createdAt      DateTime             @default(now())

  prediction ForecastPrediction @relation(fields: [predictionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([predictionId])
  @@index([sourceType, sourceId])
}

model ForecastResolution {
  id                String          @id @default(cuid())
  predictionId      String          @unique
  marketOutcome     ForecastOutcome
  brierScore        Float?
  logLoss           Float?
  calibrationBucket Decimal?        @db.Decimal(3, 1)
  resolvedAt        DateTime
  justification     String          @db.Text
  rawSettlement     Json?
  /// Resolution provenance: VENUE | OVERRIDE | BACKFILL
  source            String          @default("VENUE")
  /// Optional URL of the venue resolution page used as evidence
  sourceUrl         String?         @db.Text
  createdAt         DateTime        @default(now())

  prediction ForecastPrediction   @relation(fields: [predictionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  revisions  ResolutionRevision[]

  @@index([resolvedAt])
  @@index([calibrationBucket])
}

/// Founder-supplied override for a forecast that resolves off-venue
/// (e.g. court rulings). Override wins over the venue's reported
/// resolution; the venue discrepancy is recorded on `ResolutionMismatch`.
model ResolutionOverride {
  id            String          @id @default(cuid())
  predictionId  String          @unique
  outcome       ForecastOutcome
  resolvedAt    DateTime
  reason        String          @db.Text
  citationUrl   String          @db.Text
  founderId     String
  rawSettlement Json?
  createdAt     DateTime        @default(now())

  prediction ForecastPrediction @relation(fields: [predictionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([founderId])
  @@index([resolvedAt])
}

/// Recorded when the venue reports a resolution that disagrees with an
/// override or when the venue's resolution date is more than 7 days
/// after the prediction's target_date — the latter signals that the
/// firm's forecast was about a different question. The backfill
/// driver writes one row per disagreement and skips the resolution
/// write so a human can review it.
model ResolutionMismatch {
  id              String    @id @default(cuid())
  predictionId    String
  venue           String
  venueOutcome    String
  venueResolvedAt DateTime?
  venueSourceUrl  String?   @db.Text
  rawVenuePayload Json?
  reason          String    @db.Text
  /// "OVERRIDE_DISAGREEMENT" | "TARGET_DATE_MISMATCH"
  kind            String
  reviewedAt      DateTime?
  reviewedBy      String?
  createdAt       DateTime  @default(now())

  prediction ForecastPrediction @relation(fields: [predictionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([predictionId, createdAt])
  @@index([reviewedAt])
}

/// Append-only history of resolution updates. Existing
/// ForecastResolution rows are never overwritten — when new evidence
/// would change the resolution we instead record a revision pointing
/// at the original row and the venue payload that triggered the
/// reconsideration. Humans then decide whether to act.
model ResolutionRevision {
  id            String          @id @default(cuid())
  resolutionId  String
  newOutcome    ForecastOutcome
  newResolvedAt DateTime
  reason        String          @db.Text
  rawSettlement Json?
  /// "VENUE" | "OVERRIDE" | "BACKFILL"
  source        String
  createdAt     DateTime        @default(now())

  resolution ForecastResolution @relation(fields: [resolutionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([resolutionId, createdAt])
}

model ForecastBet {
  id               String            @id @default(cuid())
  predictionId     String
  organizationId   String
  mode             ForecastBetMode   @default(PAPER)
  exchange         ForecastExchange
  side             ForecastBetSide
  stakeUsd         Decimal           @db.Decimal(12, 2)
  entryPrice       Decimal           @db.Decimal(8, 6)
  exitPrice        Decimal?          @db.Decimal(8, 6)
  status           ForecastBetStatus
  externalOrderId  String?
  clientOrderId    String?
  settlementPnlUsd Decimal?          @db.Decimal(12, 2)
  liveAuthorizedAt DateTime?
  confirmedAt      DateTime?
  submittedAt      DateTime?
  createdAt        DateTime          @default(now())
  settledAt        DateTime?

  prediction   ForecastPrediction @relation(fields: [predictionId], references: [id])
  organization Organization       @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)

  @@index([organizationId, mode, createdAt])
  @@index([predictionId, status])
  @@index([externalOrderId])
  @@index([clientOrderId])
}

model ForecastPortfolioState {
  id                String   @id @default(cuid())
  organizationId    String
  paperBalanceUsd   Decimal  @db.Decimal(12, 2)
  liveBalanceUsd    Decimal? @db.Decimal(12, 2)
  dailyLossUsd      Decimal  @default(0) @db.Decimal(12, 2)
  dailyLossResetAt  DateTime
  killSwitchEngaged Boolean  @default(false)
  killSwitchReason  String?
  totalResolved     Int      @default(0)
  meanBrier90d      Float?
  meanLogLoss90d    Float?
  updatedAt         DateTime @updatedAt

  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)

  @@unique([organizationId])
}

model ForecastFollowUpSession {
  id                String   @id @default(cuid())
  predictionId      String
  clientFingerprint String
  createdAt         DateTime @default(now())
  lastActivityAt    DateTime @default(now())

  prediction ForecastPrediction        @relation(fields: [predictionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  messages   ForecastFollowUpMessage[]

  @@index([predictionId, lastActivityAt])
  @@index([clientFingerprint, createdAt])
}

2698	/// One unit of observable work in the noosphere pipeline. Spans nest
2699	/// under `traceId` so an upload can be followed end-to-end (ingester →
2700	/// classifier → distiller → publication). Older spans roll up to
2701	/// `MethodMetricRollup` and may be archived after 30 days; the rollup
2702	/// is the durable record.
2703	model Span {
2704	  id           String  @id
2705	  traceId      String
2706	  parentSpanId String?
2707	  name         String
2708	  status       String  @default("ok")
2709	  startedAt    DateTime
2710	  endedAt      DateTime?
2711	  durationMs   Float?
2712	  errorKind    String?
2713	  errorMessage String?
2714	  /// Sanitised attributes — never raw user text or credentials.
2715	  attrs        Json    @default("{}")
2716	  costUsd      Float   @default(0.0)
2717	  organizationId String?
2718	
2719	  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
2720	
2721	  @@index([traceId])
2722	  @@index([name, startedAt])
2723	  @@index([startedAt])
2724	  @@index([status, startedAt])
2725	}
2726	
2727	/// Per-method per-window rollup. Materialised by the same nightly job
2728	/// that computes track record. Survives span retention so the dashboard
2729	/// keeps long-horizon trendlines without scanning raw spans.
2730	model MethodMetricRollup {
2731	  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
2732	  method       String
2733	  windowStart  DateTime
2734	  windowEnd    DateTime
2735	  count        Int
2736	  errorCount   Int
2737	  p50Ms        Float
2738	  p95Ms        Float
2739	  errorRate    Float
2740	  costUsd      Float
2741	
2742	  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
2743	
2744	  @@unique([method, windowStart, windowEnd])
2745	  @@index([method, windowStart])
2746	  @@index([windowStart])
2747	}
2748	
2749	/// Threshold-based alerts (e.g. error rate > 5% sustained 15 min).
2750	/// `metric` matches a field of `MethodMetrics` (error_rate, p95_ms,
2751	/// cost_usd, count). `webhookUrl` is optional — when null the rule
2752	/// only writes to the unified attention queue.
2753	model AlertRule {
2754	  id             String  @id @default(cuid())
2755	  name           String  @unique
2756	  metric         String
2757	  threshold      Float
2758	  method         String  @default("*")
2759	  windowMinutes  Int     @default(15)
2760	  minSamples     Int     @default(5)
2761	  webhookUrl     String?
2762	  enabled        Boolean @default(true)
2763	
2764	  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
2765	  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
2766	}
2767	
2768	/// One firing of an `AlertRule`. The unified attention queue (prompt 34)
2769	/// reads from here.
2770	model AlertEvent {

2772	  ruleName   String

2775	  value      Float
2776	  threshold  Float
2777	  firedAt    DateTime
2778	  acknowledgedAt DateTime?
2779	  acknowledgedBy String?
2780	  deliveredTo Json   @default("[]")
2781	
2782	  @@index([firedAt])
2783	  @@index([ruleName, firedAt])
2784	  @@index([acknowledgedAt])
2785	}
