463	model Conclusion {
464	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
465	  organizationId String
466	
467	  /// Stable id if synced from / produced for Noosphere
468	  noosphereId String? @unique
469	  text        String
470	
471	  // ── Dedup key ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
472	  // `normalizedText` is `LOWER(TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(text, '\s+', ' ')))`
473	  // computed once at ingest time. The ingest pipeline checks for an
474	  // existing Conclusion in the same org with a matching normalizedText;
475	  // if one exists, it adds the current upload as an ADDITIONAL source
476	  // via ConclusionSource rather than inserting a duplicate row. That's
477	  // what makes deletion correct: when two uploads produced the same
478	  // claim and we delete one, the conclusion stays — it's still
479	  // supported by the other source.
480	  //
481	  // Nullable so the column can be added to an existing DB without a
482	  // rewrite; the migration back-fills every existing row. Indexed
483	  // `(organizationId, normalizedText)` because every lookup is scoped
484	  // to a single org.
485	  normalizedText         String?
486	  confidenceTier         String // firm | founder | open (ConfidenceTier)
487	  rationale              String   @default("")
488	  supportingPrincipleIds String   @default("[]") // JSON string[]
489	  evidenceChainClaimIds  String   @default("[]")
490	  dissentClaimIds        String   @default("[]")
491	  confidence             Float    @default(0)
492	  topicHint              String   @default("") // UI filter / display
493	  createdAt              DateTime @default(now())
494	
495	  /// JSON-serialized float[] embedding produced by Noosphere's sentence
496	  /// encoder at ingest time. Null for conclusions ingested before this
497	  /// column was added; the semantic explorer backfills on demand.
498	  embeddingJson String?
499	
500	  attributedFounderId     String?
501	  attributedFounder       Founder?                    @relation("ConclusionFounder", fields: [attributedFounderId], references: [id])
502	  organization            Organization                @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
503	  publicationReviews      PublicationReview[]
504	  sources                 ConclusionSource[]
505	  methodologyProfiles     MethodologyProfile[]
506	  /// Optional MQS row (1:1). Built by `noosphere mqs backfill`; re-runnable
507	  /// without rewriting the underlying methodology profile.
508	  methodologyQualityScore MethodologyQualityScore?
509	  conclusionMethods       ConclusionMethod[]
510	  domainBoundVerdicts     DomainBoundVerdict[]
511	  recalibrationOverride   RecalibrationOverride?
512	  deletionRequests        ConclusionDeletionRequest[]
513	  dashboardDismissals     DashboardDismissal[]
514	
515	  /// Conclusion edit timestamp; the public MQS pill is only shown when the
516	  /// MQS row's `scoredAt` is at least this recent.
517	  updatedAt DateTime? @updatedAt
518	
519	  @@index([organizationId])
520	  @@index([organizationId, normalizedText])
521	}
522	
523	/// Many-to-many bridge Conclusion ↔ Upload.
524	///
525	/// Every conclusion that came out of the ingest pipeline has at least
526	/// one row here tying it back to the upload it was extracted from.
527	/// When the SAME normalized text appears in multiple uploads, the
528	/// ingest pipeline reuses the existing Conclusion row and adds a new
529	/// ConclusionSource row per additional upload, so the upstream count
530	/// can drop from 2 → 1 → 0 as uploads are deleted.
531	///
532	/// Delete semantics (enforced by the app-side delete handlers, NOT by
533	/// FK cascade — we soft-delete uploads, so the FK's ON DELETE CASCADE
534	/// would not fire anyway):
535	///
536	///   * When an upload is soft-deleted, every ConclusionSource row
537	///     with that uploadId is hard-deleted.
538	///   * After that, any Conclusion with zero remaining rows is
539	///     hard-deleted too (the claim's last source is gone).
540	///   * Conclusions with sources from other uploads remain, just
541	///     with one fewer row.
542	///
543	/// The FK cascade below still serves as a safety net for the rare
544	/// case of a hard-delete (org takedown, privacy-law purge).
545	model ConclusionSource {
546	  conclusionId String
547	  uploadId     String
548	  createdAt    DateTime @default(now())
549	
550	  conclusion Conclusion @relation(fields: [conclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
551	  upload     Upload     @relation(fields: [uploadId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
552	
553	  @@id([conclusionId, uploadId])
554	  @@index([conclusionId])
555	  @@index([uploadId])
556	}
557	
558	// ── Publication (SP10) — review queue + versioned public snapshots ───────────
559	
560	/// Internal gate before a conclusion may appear on the public site.
561	model PublicationReview {
562	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
563	  organizationId String
564	  conclusionId   String
565	
566	  /// queued | in_review | needs_revision | declined | published
567	  status        String @default("queued")
568	  /// JSON: { metaAnalysisOk, adversarialEngagedOk, clarityOk, noLeakageOk }
569	  checklistJson String @default("{}")
570	  reviewerNotes String @default("")
571	  declineReason String @default("")
572	  revisionAsk   String @default("")
573	
574	  reviewerFounderId String?
575	  createdAt         DateTime @default(now())
576	  updatedAt         DateTime @updatedAt
577	
578	  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
579	  target       Conclusion   @relation(fields: [conclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
580	  reviewer     Founder?     @relation("PublicationReviewer", fields: [reviewerFounderId], references: [id])
581	
582	  @@index([organizationId])
583	  @@index([conclusionId])
584	  @@index([status])
585	}
586	
587	/// Immutable-ish public snapshot (new row per material revision).
588	model PublishedConclusion {
589	  id                 String @id @default(cuid())
590	  organizationId     String
591	  sourceConclusionId String
592	
593	  slug    String
594	  version Int    @default(1)

596	  kind    String @default("CONCLUSION")
597	
598	  /// Headline discounted confidence (SP05); stated founder/model confidence alongside in JSON.
599	  discountedConfidence      Float
600	  statedConfidence          Float  @default(0)
601	  calibrationDiscountReason String @default("")
602	
603	  /// Full page payload: evidence, exit conditions, adversarial, voice map, timeline, citations.
604	  payloadJson String @default("{}")
605	
606	  /// Zenodo DOI for this revision (minted on first publish of revision).
607	  doi            String @default("")
608	  zenodoRecordId String @default("")
609	
610	  publishedAt DateTime @default(now())
611	
612	  organization Organization          @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
613	  responses    PublicResponse[]
614	  signature    PublicationSignature?
615	
616	  @@unique([slug, version])
617	  @@index([organizationId])
618	  @@index([slug])
619	  @@index([kind, publishedAt])
620	}
621	
622	/// Cryptographic signature for a PublishedConclusion.
623	///
624	/// Generated by the noosphere CLI (`noosphere ledger sign-publication`) at
625	/// the publish moment. Private signing keys NEVER live in the web app; the
626	/// web app stores the signature artifact and serves it via
627	/// /api/public/signature/[slug]. Readers can re-verify with
628	/// `noosphere ledger verify-publication <slug>`.
629	model PublicationSignature {
630	  id                     String   @id @default(cuid())
631	  publishedConclusionId  String   @unique
632	  slug                   String
633	  version                Int
634	
635	  /// Hex-encoded SHA-256 of the canonicalized inputs (see noosphere.ledger.canonicalize).
636	  canonicalHash  String
637	  /// Hex-encoded Ed25519 signature over the canonical hash bytes.
638	  signatureHex   String
639	  /// Short fingerprint (sha256[:16]) of the verify key used to sign.
640	  keyFingerprint String
641	  /// ISO-8601 UTC timestamp at the moment of signing.
642	  signedAt       String
643	
644	  /// Full PublicationSignature artifact as JSON (canonicalInput + signature),
645	  /// served verbatim to the public verifier.
646	  payloadJson String @default("{}")
647	
648	  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
649	
650	  publishedConclusion PublishedConclusion @relation(fields: [publishedConclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
651	
652	  @@index([slug, version])
653	  @@index([keyFingerprint])
654	}
655

122	@dataclass(frozen=True)
123	class MarketDecisionTrace:
124	    action: MarketDecisionAction
125	    side: str | None
126	    confidence: float
127	    stake_recommendation_usd: float | None
128	    metrics: tuple[DecisionMetric, ...]
129	    rules: tuple[DecisionRuleResult, ...]
130	    reasons: tuple[str, ...]
131	    trace_version: str = TRACE_VERSION
132	    transfer_report: TransferReport | None = None
133	    frames: tuple[FrameResult, ...] = ()
134	    synthesis: DecisionSynthesis | None = None
135	
136	    def metric(self, name: str) -> DecisionMetric | None:
137	        for m in self.metrics:
138	            if m.name == name:
139	                return m
140	        return None
141	
142	    def frame(self, name: str) -> FrameResult | None:
143	        for f in self.frames:
144	            if f.name == name:
145	                return f
146	        return None
147	
148	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
149	        body: dict[str, Any] = {
150	            "action": self.action.value,
151	            "side": self.side,
152	            "confidence": round(float(self.confidence), 6),
153	            "stake_recommendation_usd": (
154	                None
155	                if self.stake_recommendation_usd is None
156	                else round(float(self.stake_recommendation_usd), 2)
157	            ),
158	            "metrics": [m.to_dict() for m in self.metrics],
159	            "rules": [r.to_dict() for r in self.rules],
160	            "reasons": list(self.reasons),
161	            "trace_version": self.trace_version,
162	        }
163	        if self.transfer_report is not None:
164	            body["analogical_transfer"] = self.transfer_report.to_dict()
165	        if self.frames:
166	            body["frames"] = [f.to_dict() for f in self.frames]
167	        if self.synthesis is not None:
168	            body["synthesis"] = self.synthesis.to_dict()
169	        return body
170

177	    if value.tzinfo is None:
178	        return value.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
179	    return value.astimezone(timezone.utc)

file.content:
# explicit rule in :mod:`noosphere.decisions.synthesis`. Frames may
    # *downgrade* the action when a HARD_STOP fires for a signal the
    # rule graph above does not already cover (e.g. caller-supplied
    # incentive_signals). They never escalate.
    frame_ctx = context_from_decision_metrics(
        side=side,
        metrics=metrics,
        transfer_report=transfer_report,
        position_state=position_state,
        incentive_signals=incentive_signals,
        coordination_signals=coordination_signals,
        reflexive_signals=reflexive_signals,
        market_closed=closed,
    )
    frame_results = run_frames(frame_ctx)
    synthesis = synthesize(frame_results, default_side=side)

    if (
        synthesis.action == SynthesisAction.ABSTAIN
        and synthesis.hard_stop_frames
        and action in (MarketDecisionAction.PAPER_TRADE, MarketDecisionAction.LIVE_CANDIDATE)
    ):
        reasons.append(
            "multi-frame synthesis HARD_STOP from "
            + ", ".join(synthesis.hard_stop_frames)
            + " → abstain"
        )
        action = MarketDecisionAction.ABSTAIN
        side = None
    elif (
        synthesis.action == SynthesisAction.EXIT
        and action != MarketDecisionAction.ABSTAIN
    ):
        # An EXIT signal from a frame supersedes paper/live bucket
        # actions (the principal-agent frame raises EXIT on a revoked
        # principle; the rule-graph position overlay does the same,
        # so this branch is just defense-in-depth).
        if action != MarketDecisionAction.EXIT:
            reasons.append("multi-frame synthesis → exit overrides bucket action")
        action = MarketDecisionAction.EXIT
        side = None

    stake_recommendation = _stake_recommendation(paper_bet, edge_metric, liquidity_metric)
    if action not in (MarketDecisionAction.PAPER_TRADE, MarketDecisionAction.LIVE_CANDIDATE):
        stake_recommendation = None

    return MarketDecisionTrace(
        action=action,
        side=side,
        confidence=confidence_scalar,
        stake_recommendation_usd=stake_recommendation,
        metrics=metrics,
        rules=rules,
        reasons=tuple(reasons),
        transfer_report=transfer_report,
        frames=frame_results,
        synthesis=synthesis,
    )


def decision_trace_to_dict(trace: MarketDecisionTrace) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Public stable JSON shape for UI/API consumption."""
    return trace.to_dict()


def decision_trace_gate_entries(trace: MarketDecisionTrace) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """Render the rule-graph firings in the legacy ``gate_results`` shape.

    The forecast_generator already persists a ``gate_results`` list with
    ``gateName/passed/reason`` keys; the rule graph's threshold/veto
    nodes naturally project onto the same shape so downstream
    consumers (operator page, e2e tests) keep working without a
    schema migration.
    """
    return [
        {
            "gateName": rule.name,
            "passed": bool(rule.passed),
            "reason": rule.detail,
        }
        for rule in trace.rules
        if rule.fired
    ]

    "DecisionMetric",
    "DecisionRuleResult",
    "MarketDecisionAction",
    "MarketDecisionTrace",

3	The rule, in priority order:
4	
5	1. Any :class:`FrameVerdict.HARD_STOP` → :class:`SynthesisAction.ABSTAIN`,
6	   regardless of how many other frames agreed. (Hard stops are
7	   categorical refusals; counting them against majority votes would
8	   defeat their purpose.)
9	2. Any :class:`FrameVerdict.EXIT` from a position-aware frame →
10	   :class:`SynthesisAction.EXIT`. ``EXIT`` overrides ``REDUCE``
11	   and ``HEDGE``.

13	   :class:`SynthesisAction.REDUCE`.

15	5. If a configurable fraction of frames flag ``assumptions_stable=False``
16	   → :class:`SynthesisAction.ABSTAIN` (assumptions too unstable).
17	6. If a strict majority of *eligible* frames vote ``SUPPORT`` and no
18	   frame voted ``ABSTAIN`` with a hard reason → :class:`SynthesisAction.SUPPORT`
19	   (strong action — the caller decides whether to paper-trade or live).
20	7. Otherwise → :class:`SynthesisAction.WATCH` if any frame had a

file.content:
"""Synthesis rule — combine multiple frame verdicts into one action.

The rule, in priority order:

1. Any :class:`FrameVerdict.HARD_STOP` → :class:`SynthesisAction.ABSTAIN`,
   regardless of how many other frames agreed. (Hard stops are
   categorical refusals; counting them against majority votes would
   defeat their purpose.)
2. Any :class:`FrameVerdict.EXIT` from a position-aware frame →
   :class:`SynthesisAction.EXIT`. ``EXIT`` overrides ``REDUCE``
   and ``HEDGE``.
3. Any :class:`FrameVerdict.REDUCE` (and no EXIT/HARD_STOP) →
   :class:`SynthesisAction.REDUCE`.
4. ``HEDGE`` (rare in v1) → :class:`SynthesisAction.HEDGE`.
5. If a configurable fraction of frames flag ``assumptions_stable=False``
   → :class:`SynthesisAction.ABSTAIN` (assumptions too unstable).
6. If a strict majority of *eligible* frames vote ``SUPPORT`` and no
   frame voted ``ABSTAIN`` with a hard reason → :class:`SynthesisAction.SUPPORT`
   (strong action — the caller decides whether to paper-trade or live).
7. Otherwise → :class:`SynthesisAction.WATCH` if any frame had a
   relevant signal, else :class:`SynthesisAction.ABSTAIN`.

The result records *which* frames agreed, *which* dissented, and the
synthesis path taken — so a reader can inspect the trace and see the
reasoning rather than just the verdict.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections import Counter
from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum
from typing import Any, Sequence

from noosphere.decisions.frames import FrameResult, FrameVerdict


SYNTHESIS_VERSION = "decision_synthesis@v1"


# SUPPORT requires *all* assumption-stable frames to vote SUPPORT.
# A single WATCH/REDUCE among the stable frames downgrades to WATCH;
# this is the synthesis-level enforcement of "strong action only when
# multiple frames agree and no frame dissents". Unstable frames are
# treated as "no signal" — they neither support nor block.
MIN_ELIGIBLE_FRAMES_FOR_SUPPORT = 3

# The set of verdicts that count as "this frame had a real signal".
# WATCH counts as a signal; ABSTAIN does not.
RELEVANT_SIGNAL_VERDICTS = frozenset(
    {
        FrameVerdict.SUPPORT,
        FrameVerdict.WATCH,
        FrameVerdict.REDUCE,
        FrameVerdict.EXIT,
        FrameVerdict.HEDGE,
        FrameVerdict.HARD_STOP,
    }
)


class SynthesisAction(str, Enum):
    """Final synthesized action.

    Aligned with :class:`noosphere.forecasts.decision_metrics.MarketDecisionAction`
    so the synthesis can be quoted in the market decision trace
    without re-mapping. ``SUPPORT`` is the synthesis-level analog of
    "the lenses agree the trade is warranted"; the *capital* layer
    (paper vs live) is decided downstream from the existing rule
    graph, not here.
    """

    SUPPORT = "SUPPORT"
    WATCH = "WATCH"
    ABSTAIN = "ABSTAIN"
    REDUCE = "REDUCE"
    EXIT = "EXIT"
    HEDGE = "HEDGE"

  };
}

content.text:
Both `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm run build` pass cleanly.

## Summary

Made cases, abstract principles, case→principle links, principle transfer, and decision-frame traces legible across the founder surfaces.

**Forecast trace types and normalizer** (`src/lib/forecastsTypes.ts`, `src/lib/forecastPortfolioData.ts`)
- Extended `DecisionTrace` with `frames: DecisionFrame[]`, `synthesis: DecisionSynthesis | null`, and `analogicalTransfer: AnalogicalTransferReport | null` so the data the Python pipeline already emits in `ForecastTrace.modelOutput` becomes UI-addressable.
- Added typed normalizers for frame verdicts, synthesis, and transfer recommendations.

**DecisionTracePanel** (`forecasts/portfolio/DecisionTracePanel.tsx`)
- New collapsed sections render the per-frame verdicts (with assumption stability), the synthesis verdict + agreement, and the empirical-transfer report with stance/confidence/reasons per recommendation.

**Operator confirmations** (`forecasts/operator/PendingConfirmations.tsx`)
- Inline `FrameStrip` and `TransferStrip` surface the frame verdicts and transfer recommendations right where operators authorize bets.

**Knowledge tabs** (`knowledge/page.tsx`)
- Added `Principles` and `Cases` tabs with new server components `PrinciplesTab.tsx` (lists accepted / queued / public-visible counts + top accepted, with empty states for the transfer graph) and `CasesTab.tsx` (case-kind taxonomy + empty states naming that case rows aren't persisted yet).

**Transcript page** (`transcripts/[uploadId]/page.tsx`, `CasesAndPrinciplesPanel.tsx`)
- Classifies conclusions sourced from the upload as `abstract_principle` (any accepted `Principle.cited/cluster` references the conclusion), `empirical` (firm/founder tier), or `unclassified` (open/speculative), and renders them grouped, with linked-principle chips. Empty-state card explains that typed empirical case rows aren't persisted yet.

**Conclusion detail** (`conclusions/[id]/page.tsx`)
- New `Kind` chip (Abstract principle / Empirical observation / Decision rule / Unclassified) with tooltip explaining the classification.
- New "Abstracted into" pill row linking to accepted principles that cite or cluster the conclusion.
- New `CasesSection` in the Overview tab with an honest empty state for supporting/contradicting cases.

**Test fixture** (`__tests__/forecastPortfolioView.test.tsx`)
- Updated to populate the now-required `frames`, `synthesis`, and `analogicalTransfer` fields.

Public-source visibility rules are preserved — every new surface lives under `(authed)` and reads through `requireTenantContext` / `getFounder`. No new fetch path bypasses the existing visibility filters. All new disclosures default to closed and read precomputed data, so no analysis-bound work is introduced. Note: I did not browser-check the routes; the verification I ran is `npx tsc --noEmit` (clean) and `npm run build` (clean — 75 static pages generated).

Both `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm run build` pass cleanly.

## Summary

Made cases, abstract principles, case→principle links, principle transfer, and decision-frame traces legible across the founder surfaces.

**Forecast trace types and normalizer** (`src/lib/forecastsTypes.ts`, `src/lib/forecastPortfolioData.ts`)
- Extended `DecisionTrace` with `frames: DecisionFrame[]`, `synthesis: DecisionSynthesis | null`, and `analogicalTransfer: AnalogicalTransferReport | null` so the data the Python pipeline already emits in `ForecastTrace.modelOutput` becomes UI-addressable.
- Added typed normalizers for frame verdicts, synthesis, and transfer recommendations.

**DecisionTracePanel** (`forecasts/portfolio/DecisionTracePanel.tsx`)
- New collapsed sections render the per-frame verdicts (with assumption stability), the synthesis verdict + agreement, and the empirical-transfer report with stance/confidence/reasons per recommendation.

**Operator confirmations** (`forecasts/operator/PendingConfirmations.tsx`)
- Inline `FrameStrip` and `TransferStrip` surface the frame verdicts and transfer recommendations right where operators authorize bets.

**Knowledge tabs** (`knowledge/page.tsx`)
- Added `Principles` and `Cases` tabs with new server components `PrinciplesTab.tsx` (lists accepted / queued / public-visible counts + top accepted, with empty states for the transfer graph) and `CasesTab.tsx` (case-kind taxonomy + empty states naming that case rows aren't persisted yet).

**Transcript page** (`transcripts/[uploadId]/page.tsx`, `CasesAndPrinciplesPanel.tsx`)
- Classifies conclusions sourced from the upload as `abstract_principle` (any accepted `Principle.cited/cluster` references the conclusion), `empirical` (firm/founder tier), or `unclassified` (open/speculative), and renders them grouped, with linked-principle chips. Empty-state card explains that typed empirical case rows aren't persisted yet.

**Conclusion detail** (`conclusions/[id]/page.tsx`)
- New `Kind` chip (Abstract principle / Empirical observation / Decision rule / Unclassified) with tooltip explaining the classification.
- New "Abstracted into" pill row linking to accepted principles that cite or cluster the conclusion.
- New `CasesSection` in the Overview tab with an honest empty state for supporting/contradicting cases.

**Test fixture** (`__tests__/forecastPortfolioView.test.tsx`)
- Updated to populate the now-required `frames`, `synthesis`, and `analogicalTransfer` fields.

Public-source visibility rules are preserved — every new surface lives under `(authed)` and reads through `requireTenantContext` / `getFounder`. No new fetch path bypasses the existing visibility filters. All new disclosures default to closed and read precomputed data, so no analysis-bound work is introduced. Note: I did not browser-check the routes; the verification I ran is `npx tsc --noEmit` (clean) and `npm run build` (clean — 75 static pages generated).
